title: Battle of Tiems

{{Quote|Rieekan had beaten the enemy commander's spirit. By Rieekan's brilliant tactics and his troopers' flawless execution of those tactics, he had made his enemy doubt himself and his troops. The Imperial general did not attack—''at three to one odds''—because ''he'' was whipped.|General Crix Madine on the Battle of Tiems during a lecture on strategy and tactics|<ref name="RASB1" />}}
The '''Battle of Tiems''' was an [[Ground warfare/Legends|engagement]] fought between the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], in the area surrounding the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Tiems]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tiems (planet)|of the same name]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] led a detachment of three [[Alliance Army/Legends|Alliance Army]] [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] [[Regiment/Legends|regiments]] to liberate thousands of Alliance [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] from Imperial [[Prison/Legends|facilities]] located in Tiems. After successfully completing the assignment, their extraction was delayed as the [[Transport/Legends|transports]] were held up by enemy [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] forces.

Forced to defend a fixed position—the landing zone for their extraction—against a numerically superior enemy, Rieekan employed a mobile defense. By knowing that his Imperial [[Unidentified Imperial general (Tiems)|counterpart]] would concentrate his full assault on the weakest part of the Alliance defensive line, Rieekan drew the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial forces]] into an effective trap, [[Death/Legends|destroying]] half their number. The remaining Imperial [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] withdrew to Tiems to regroup and reinforce, although by the [[Time/Legends|time]] the Imperial [[Commander/Legends|commander]] mustered his forces, the Alliance soldiers had been extracted from the planet. Following the battle, the engagement was used by [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]] General [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] as the perfect example of the mobile defense tactic during orientation briefings to new Rebel ground forces commanders.

==Prelude==
Around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], having learned that several thousand [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] personnel were being held by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[Prison/Legends|prisons]]<ref name="RASB1" /> on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tiems (planet)|Tiems]]<ref name="Tiems-confirm" /> in the [[Tiems system/Legends|system of the same name]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]' [[Belderone sector/Legends|Belderone sector]],<ref name="TEA-OC" /> the Alliance organized a mission to liberate them. [[Commander/Legends|Commanded]] by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]], a detachment of three [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] [[Regiment/Legends|regiments]] were assigned to the operation, backed up by several [[Transport/Legends|transports]] to ferry the [[Troop/Legends|troops]] to the planet and then retrieve them once they had freed the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]]. The Imperial forces on the planet included four regiments of [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial soldiers]], four regiments of indigenous [[Trooper/Legends|troops]], and had [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] support available to them.<ref name="RASB1" />

==The battle==
[[File:Tiems_battle_progression.jpg|thumb|left|Progression of the Battle of Tiems, including key]]
Rieekan's [[Alliance Army/Legends|Alliance Army]] regiments landed on the planet and attacked the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Tiems]] where the prisons were located, successfully destroying a [[Tiems power station|power station]] and freeing all the incarcerated Rebel personnel. Following the mission, Rieekan and his troops were to head down the [[Tiems Road]] to a landing zone on the other side of a [[Unidentified town (Tiems)|small town]] for extraction. However, the transports assigned to extract Rieekan, his troops, and the prisoners off-planet were delayed by enemy starfighter action. Forced to defend the designated landing zone against an Imperial counter-attack—which would be numerically superior—until the rescue could be effected, Rieekan chose to employ a mobile defense and draw the Imperials into a trap.<ref name="RASB1" />

Between the city of Tiems and the landing zone was the Tiems Road, flanked on the right by the [[Blue River]] and on the left by the [[Gopps Forest]]. A small town sat on a crossroads of the Tiems Road, between Tiems itself and the Alliance landing zone. Rieekan deployed his troops in a defensive line, strung out in the open terrain surrounding the Tiems Road, and using the cover of the [[Tree/Legends|forest]] and river on the flanks. His intent was to make it appear that the line was held by all three regiments of [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] under his command, when in reality it was only held by the equivalent of one regiment. Rieekan, aware that Imperial doctrine called for maximum force to be applied to the weakest part of the enemy's line, knew that his [[Unidentified Imperial general (Tiems)|Imperial counterpart]] would concentrate his assault on the center of the Alliance line, which was without forest or river cover. The other two regiments worth of troops had been positioned to the rear of the line, hidden in cover by the forest and the river on either side of the road near the town on the crossroads.<ref name="RASB1" />

At 1330 [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]], local [[Time/Legends|time]], the Imperial General ordered his troops to assault the Alliance line. Under his command were four regiments of infantry. The [[1st Regiment|1st]] and [[3rd Regiment]]s assaulted the flanks of the Alliance line, holding the [[Battalion/Legends|battalions]] manning the line in place. The [[2nd Regiment (Tiems)|2nd Regiment]] attacked the center of the line—which, without cover from the forest or river, was the weakest point of the Alliance line—as doctrine required. The [[4th Regiment]] remained in reserve, ready to exploit any breakthroughs. The [[Ground warfare/Legends|fighting]] was fierce all along the line, with the Alliance battalions assigned to the defense holding their positions against a superior force for over two hours to allow the trap to develop. The troops on the flanks had orders to hold firm, while the troops in the center had orders to retreat at the proper time and fall back to the town on the crossroads.<ref name="RASB1" />

On Rieekan's order, the troops in the center fell back in good order toward the town located between their position and the landing zone and dug in. Both the 2nd and 4th Imperial regiments followed the Alliance forces, straight between the two hidden regiments along the bank of the Blue River and in the Gopps Forest. The hidden Alliance forces attacked the Imperial regiments on the flanks, while the battalions that had fallen back to the town counter-attacked from the front. The Alliance forces surrounded them and almost completely [[Death/Legends|destroyed]] both Imperial formations.<ref name="RASB1" />

After the ambush, the Imperial survivors fell back to Tiems in disorder to await reinforcements. An entire division of native soldiers—totaling four regiments—arrived at the city twenty-four hours later. The Imperial commander waited a further twelve hours before launching another offensive to allow the weather to clear enough so that his attack could be supported by air cover from a local base. By that time, General Rieekan's detachment and the prisoners had been successfully extracted from the planet, with their mission accomplished.<ref name="RASB1" />

==Aftermath==
General Rieekan's [[After-action report/Legends|After Action Report]] made it clear that the victory could not have been achieved without the well trained, high-motivated and well-led Alliance troops, who held out against superior Imperial forces for hours while the trap was readied. In addition, Rieekan's intimate knowledge of Imperial doctrine, and knowing that his Imperial counterpart would adhere to those rules of combat, allowed him to design a trap for which the Imperial commander would no doubt fall.<ref name="RASB1" />

Rieekan also proved at Tiems that one did not have to annihilate the enemy to defeat them. The Imperial forces started the battle with four regiments to the Alliance's three. The Imperials lost two regiments in the ambush, while the Alliance lost the equivalent of one regiment setting up the trap. When the Imperial commander was reinforced, he had six regiments under his command, compared to the Alliance's two. The Imperials had a three-to-one advantage, but delayed their attack to wait for air cover—which was too late.<ref name="RASB1" />

Following the battle, General [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]], commander of the [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]], used the battle as a textbook example of the mobile defense in orientations given to new Alliance ground commanders. He dissected the battle plan, and urged the new commanders to study the Battle of Tiems so they could use the lessons it taught in their own combat theaters. Among the lessons he hoped the new commanders would learn was that one did not have to destroy an enemy force to beat it, and that making the enemy commander fear them through the use of superior tactics and strategy would give them the advantage.<ref name="RASB1" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Re Tiems, yes, city of Tiems on planet of same name. Thinking is the Rebel Sourcebook had ample opportunity to name a planet but didn't, and 'Battle of Tiems' implies a larger conflict, so Tiems it is.|Jason Fry addresses the listing of a "Tiems system" in ''The Essential Atlas''|<ref name="Tiems-confirm" />}}
The Battle of Tiems was first featured in Chapter 6 of the ''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'', published in [[1990]]. It was subsequently reprinted in the [[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'', Second Edition]] in [[1994]]. In the sourcebook, Tiems is only referred to as a city where the battle takes place, with no mention of the planet on which the city is located. In [[2009]], ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' included a reference to a "Tiems [[Star system/Legends|system]]" in the appendix, indicating the existence of the planet named Tiems. ''The Essential Atlas'' author [[Jason Fry]] confirmed that was the intention when compiling the Atlas, stating that he felt that the original sourcebook had an opportunity to name the planet and never did, while the name "Battle of Tiems" indicated that there was a larger conflict ongoing.<ref name="Tiems-confirm" />
